author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

ccel.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

title: Chapter X. 1–10.

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Our Lord’s discourse to the Jews began in connection with the man who was born blind and was restored to sight.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Your Charity therefore ought to know and be advised that today’s lesson is interwoven with that one.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For when the Lord had said, “For judgment I am come into this world; that they who see not might see, and they who see might be made blind,”—which, on the occasion of its reading, we expounded according to our ability,—some of the Pharisees said, “Are we blind also?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To whom He replied, “If ye were blind, ye should have no sin: but now ye say, We see; [therefore] your sin remaineth.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To these words He added what we have been hearing today when the lesson was read.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Verily, verily, I say unto you, He that entereth not by the door into the sheepfold, but climbeth up some other way, the same is a thief and a robber.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For they declared that they were not blind; yet could they see only by being the sheep of Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Whence claimed they possession of the light, who were acting as thieves against the day?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Because, then, of their vain and proud and incurable arrogance, did the Lord Jesus subjoin these words, wherein He has given us also salutary lessons, if we lay them to heart.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For there are many who, according to a custom of this life, are called good people,—good men, good women, innocent, and observers as it were of what is commanded in the law; paying respect to their parents, abstaining from adultery, doing no murder, committing no theft, giving no false witness against any one, and observing all else that the law requires—yet are not Christians; and for the most part ask boastfully, like these men, “Are we blind also?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But just because all these things that they do, and know not to what end they should have reference, they do to no purpose, the Lord has set forth in today’s lesson the similitude of His own flock, and of the door that leads into the sheepfold.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Pagans may say, then, We live well.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If they enter not by the door, what good will that do them, whereof they boast?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For to this end ought good living to benefit every one, that it may be given him to live for ever: for to whomsoever eternal life is not given, of what benefit is the living well?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For they ought not to be spoken of as even living well, who either from blindness know not the end of a right life, or in their pride despise it.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But no one has the true and certain hope of living always, unless he know the life, that it is Christ; and enter by the gate into the sheepfold.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Such, accordingly, for the most part seek to persuade men to live well, and yet not to be Christians.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: By another way they wish to climb up, to steal and to kill, not as the shepherd, to preserve and to save.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And thus there have been certain philosophers, holding many subtle discussions about the virtues and the vices, dividing, defining, drawing out to their close the most acute processes of reasoning, filling books, brandishing their wisdom with rattling jaws; who would even dare to say to people, Follow us, keep to our sect, if you would live happily.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But they had not entered by the door: they wished to destroy, to slay, and to murder.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What shall I say of such?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Look, the Pharisees themselves were in the habit of reading, and in what they read, their voices re-echoed the Christ, they hoped He would come, and recognized Him not when present; they boasted, even they, of being amongst those who saw, that is, among the wise, and they disowned the Christ, and entered not in by the door.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore would such also, if they chanced to seduce any, seduce them to be slaughtered and murdered, not to be brought into liberty.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us leave these also to themselves, and look at those who glory in the name of Christ Himself, and see whether even they perchance are entering in by the door.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For there are countless numbers who not only boast that they see, but would have it appear that they are enlightened by Christ; yet are they heretics.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Have even they somehow entered by the gate?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Sabellius says, He who is the Son is Himself the Father; but if the Son, then is there no Father.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He enters not by the door, who asserts that the Son is the Father.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Arius says, The Father is one thing, the Son is another thing.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He would say rightly if he said, Another person; but not another thing.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For when he says, Another thing, he contradicts Him who says in his hearing, “I and my Father are One.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Neither does he therefore enter by the door; for he preaches a Christ such as he fabricates for himself, not such as the truth declares Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou hast the name, thou hast not the reality.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Christ is the name of something; keep hold of the thing itself, if thou wouldst benefit by the name.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Another, I know not from whence, says with Photinus, Christ is mere man; He is not God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He enters not in by the door, for Christ is both man and God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But why need I make many references, and enumerate the many vanities of heretics?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Keep hold of this, that Christ’s sheepfold is the Catholic Church.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Whoever would enter the sheepfold, let him enter by the door, let him preach the true Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Not only let him preach the true Christ, but seek Christ’s glory, not his own; for many, by seeking their own glory, have scattered Christ’s sheep, instead of gathering them.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For Christ the Lord is a low gateway: he who enters by this gateway must humble himself, that he may be able to enter with head unharmed.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But he that humbleth not, but exalteth himself, wishes to climb over the wall; and he that climbeth over the wall, is exalted only to fall.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thus far, however, the Lord Jesus speaks in covert language; not as yet is He understood.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He names the door, He names the sheepfold, He names the sheep: all this He sets forth, but does not yet explain.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us read on then, for He is coming to those words, wherein He may think proper to give us some explanation of what He has said; from the explanation of which He will perhaps enable us to understand also what He has not explained.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For He gives us what is plain, for food; what is obscure, for exercise.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “He that entereth not by the door into the sheepfold, but climbeth up some other way.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Woe to the wretch, for he is sure to fall!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let him then be humble, let him enter by the door: let him walk on the level ground, and he shall not stumble.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “The same,” He says, “is a thief and a robber.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The sheep of another he desires to call his own sheep,—his own, that is, as carried off by stealth, for the purpose, not of saving, but of slaying them.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore is he a thief, because what is another’s he calls his own; a robber, because what he has stolen he also kills.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “But he that entereth in by the door is the shepherd of the sheep: to him the porter openeth.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Concerning this porter we shall make inquiry, when we have heard of the Lord Himself what is the door and who is the shepherd.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “And the sheep hear his voice: and he calleth his own sheep by name.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For He has their names written in the book of life.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “He calleth his own sheep by name.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hence, says the apostle, “The Lord knoweth them that are His.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “And he leadeth them out.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And when he putteth forth his own sheep, he goeth before them, and the sheep follow him: for they know his voice.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And a stranger do they not follow, but do flee from him: for they know not the voice of strangers.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: These are veiled words, full of topics of inquiry, pregnant with sacramental signs.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us follow then, and listen to the Master as He makes some opening into these obscurities; and perhaps by the opening He makes, He will cause us to enter.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “This parable spake Jesus unto them; but they understood not what He spake unto them.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Nor we also, perhaps.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What, then, is the difference between them and us, before even we can understand these words?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This, that we on our part knock, that it may be opened unto us; while they, by disowning Christ, refused to enter for salvation, and preferred remaining outside to be destroyed.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In as far, then, as we listen to these words with a pious mind, in as far as, before we understand them, we believe them to be true and divine, we stand at a great distance from these men.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For when two persons are listening to the words of the gospel, the one impious, the other pious, and some of these are such as neither perhaps understands, the one says, It has said nothing; the other says, It has said the truth, and what it has said is good, but we do not understand it.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This latter, because he believes, now knocks, that he may be worthy to have it opened up to him, if he continue knocking; but the other still hears the words, “If ye believe not, ye shall not understand.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why do I draw your attention to this?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Even for this reason, that when I have explained as I can these obscure words, or, because of their great abstruseness, I have either myself failed to arrive at an understanding of them, or wanted the faculty of explaining what I do understand, or every one has been so dull as not to follow me, even when I give the explanation, yet should he not despair of himself; but continue in faith, walk on in the way, and hear the apostle saying, “And if in anything ye be otherwise minded, God shall reveal even this unto you.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Nevertheless whereto we have already attained, let us walk therein.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us begin, then, with hearing His exposition of what we have heard Him propounding.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Then said Jesus unto them again, Verily, verily, I say unto you, I am the door of the sheep.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: See, He has opened the very door which was shut in His former description.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He Himself is the door.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We have come to know it; let us enter, or rejoice that we are already within.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “All that ever came are thieves and robbers.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is this, Lord, “All that ever came”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How so hast Thou not come?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But understand; I said, “All that ever came,” meaning, of course, exclusive of myself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us recollect then.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Before His coming came the prophets: were they thieves and robbers?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They did not come apart from Him, for they came with Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When about to come, He sent heralds, but retained possession of the hearts of His messengers.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Do you wish to know that they came with Him, who is Himself ever existent?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Certainly He assumed human flesh at the time appointed.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But what means that “ever”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “In the beginning was the Word.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: With Him, therefore, came those who came with the word of God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “I am,” said He, “the way, and the truth, and the life.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If He is the truth, with Him came those who were truthful.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: As many, therefore, as were apart from Him, were “thieves and robbers,” that is, had come to steal and to destroy.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “But the sheep did not hear them.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This is a more important point, “the sheep did not hear them.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Before the advent of our Lord Jesus Christ, when He came in humility in the flesh, righteous men preceded, believing in the same way in Him who was to come, as we believe in Him who has come.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Times vary, but not faith.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For verbs themselves also vary with the tense, when they are variously declined.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He is to come, has one sound; He has come, has another: there is a change in the sound between He is to come, and He has come: yet the same faith unites both,—both those who believed that He would come, and those who have believed that He is come.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: At different times, indeed, but by the one doorway of faith, that is, by Christ, do we see that both have entered.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We believe that the Lord Jesus Christ was born of the Virgin, that He came in the flesh, suffered, rose again, ascended into heaven: all this, just as you hear verbs of the past tense, we believe to be already fulfilled.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In that faith a partnership is also held with us by those fathers who believed that He would be born of the Virgin, would suffer, would rise again, would ascend into heaven; for to such the apostle pointed when he said, “But we having the same spirit of faith, according as it is written, I believed, and therefore have I spoken; we also believe, and therefore speak.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The prophet said, “I believed, therefore have I spoken:” the apostle says, “We also believe, and therefore speak.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But to let you know that their faith is one, listen to him saying, “Having the same spirit of faith, we also believe.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: So also in another place, “For I would not have you ignorant, brethren, how that all our fathers were under the cloud, and all passed through the sea: and were all baptized unto Moses in the cloud and in the sea; and did all eat the same spiritual meat, and did all drink the same spiritual drink.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Red Sea signifies baptism; Moses, their leader through the Red Sea, signifies Christ; the people, who passed through, signify believers; the death of the Egyptians signifies the abolition of sins.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Under different signs there is the same faith.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is with different signs as with different words [verbs]; for verbs change their sounds through the tenses, and verbs are indeed nothing else than signs.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For they are words because of what they signify: take away the meaning from a word, and it becomes a senseless sound.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: All, therefore, have become signs.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Was not the same faith theirs by whom these signs were employed, and by whom were foretold in prophecy the very things which we believe?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Certainly it was: but they believed that they were yet to come, and we, that they have come.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In like manner does he also say, “They all drank the same spiritual drink;” “the same spiritual,” for it was not the same material [drink].

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For what was it they drank?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “For they drank of the spiritual Rock that followed them; and that Rock was Christ.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: See, then, how that while the faith remained, the signs were varied.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: There the rock was Christ; to us that is Christ which is placed on the altar of God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And they, as a great sacramental sign of the same Christ, drank the water flowing from the rock: what we drink is known to believers.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If one’s thoughts turn to the visible form, the thing is different; if to the meaning that addresses the understanding, they drank the same spiritual drink.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: As many, then, at that time as believed, whether Abraham, or Isaac, or Jacob, or Moses, or the other patriarchs or prophets who foretold of Christ, were sheep, and heard Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: His voice, and not another’s, did they hear.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Judge was present in the person of the Crier.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For even when the judge speaks through the crier, the clerk does not make it, The crier said; but the judge said.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But others there are whom the sheep did not hear, in whom Christ’s voice had no place,—wanderers, uttering falsehoods, prating inanities, fabricating vanities, misleading the miserable.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why is it, then, that I have said, This is a more important point?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is there about it obscure and difficult to understand?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Listen, I beseech you.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: See, the Lord Jesus Christ Himself came and preached.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Much more surely was that the Shepherd’s voice which was uttered by the very mouth of the Shepherd.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if the Shepherd’s voice came through the prophets, how much more did the Shepherd’s own tongue give utterance to the Shepherd’s voice?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Yet all did not hear Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But what are we to think?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Those who did hear, were they sheep?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Judas heard, and was a wolf: he followed, but, clad in sheep-skin, he was laying snares for the Shepherd.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Some, again, of those who crucified Christ did not hear, and yet were sheep; for such He saw in the crowd when He said, “When ye have lifted up the Son of man, then shall ye know that I am He.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Now, how is this question to be solved?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They that are not sheep do hear, and they that are sheep do not hear.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Some, who are wolves, follow the Shepherd’s voice; and some, that are sheep, contradict it.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Last of all, the sheep slay the Shepherd.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The point is solved; for some one in reply says, But when they did not hear, as yet they were not sheep, they were then wolves: the voice, when it was heard, changed them, and out of wolves transformed them into sheep; and so, when they became sheep, they heard, and found the Shepherd, and followed Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They built their hopes on the Shepherd’s promises, because they obeyed His precepts.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That question has been solved in a way, and perhaps satisfies every one.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But I bare still a subject of concern, and what concerns me I shall impart to you, that, in some sort inquiring together, I may through His revelation be found worthy with you to attain the solution.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hear, then, what it is that moves me.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: By the Prophet Ezekiel the Lord rebukes the shepherds, and among other things says of the sheep, “The wandering sheep have ye not recalled.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He both declares it a wanderer, and calls it a sheep.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If, while wandering, it was a sheep, whose voice was it hearing to lead it astray?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For doubtless it would not be straying were it hearing the shepherd’s voice: but it strayed just because it heard another’s voice; it heard the voice of the thief and the robber.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Surely the sheep do not hear the voice of robbers.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Those that came,” He said,—and we are to understand, apart from me,—that is, “those that came apart from me are thieves and robbers, and the sheep did not hear them.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Lord, if the sheep did not hear them, how can the sheep wander?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If the sheep hear only Thee, and Thou art the truth, whoever heareth the truth cannot certainly fall into error.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But they err, and are called sheep.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if, in the very midst of their wandering, they were not called sheep, it would not be said by Ezekiel, “The wandering sheep have ye not recalled.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How is it at the same time a wanderer and a sheep?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Has it heard the voice of another?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Surely “the sheep did not hear them.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Accordingly many are just now being gathered into Christ’s fold, and from being heretics are becoming catholics.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They are rescued from the thieves, and restored to the shepherds: and sometimes they murmur, and become wearied of Him that calls them back, and have no true knowledge of him that would murder them; nevertheless also, when, after a struggle, those have come who are sheep, they recognize the Shepherd’s voice, and are glad they have come, and are ashamed of their wandering.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When, then, they were glorying in that state of error as in the truth, and were certainly not hearing the Shepherd’s voice, but were following another, were they sheep, or were they not?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If they were sheep, how can it be the case that the sheep do not listen to aliens?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If they were not sheep, wherefore the rebuke addressed to those to whom it is said, “The wandering sheep have ye not recalled”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In the case also of those already become catholic Christians, and believers of good promise, evils sometimes occur: they are seduced into error, and after their error are restored.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When they were thus seduced, and were rebaptized, or after the companionship of the Lord’s fold were turned back again into their former error, were they sheep, or were they not?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Certainly they were catholics.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If they were faithful catholics, they were sheep.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If they were sheep, how was it that they could listen to the voice of a stranger when the Lord saith, “The sheep did not hear them”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: You hear, brethren, the great importance of the question.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I say then, “The Lord knoweth them that are His.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He knoweth those who were foreknown, He knoweth those who were predestinated; because it is said of Him, “For whom He did foreknow, He also did predestinate to be conformed to the image of His Son, that He might be the firstborn among many brethren.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Moreover, whom He did predestinate, them He also called; and whom He called, them He also justified; and whom He justified, them He also glorified.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If God be for us, who can be against us?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Add to this: “He that spared not His own Son, but delivered Him up for us all, how hath He not with Him also freely given us all things?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But what “us”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Those who are foreknown, predestinated, justified, glorified; regarding whom there follows, “Who shall lay anything to the charge of God’s elect?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore “the Lord knoweth them that are His;” they are the sheep.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Such sometimes do not know themselves, but the Shepherd knoweth them, according to this predestination, this foreknowledge of God, according to the election of the sheep before the foundation of the world: for so saith also the apostle, “According as He hath chosen us in Him before the foundation of the world.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: According, then, to this divine foreknowledge and predestination, how many sheep are outside, how many wolves within! and how many sheep are inside, how many wolves without!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How many are now living in wantonness who will yet be chaste! how many are blaspheming Christ who will yet believe in Him! how many are giving themselves to drunkenness who will yet be sober! how many are preying on other people property who will yet freely give of their own!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Nevertheless at present they are hearing the voice of another, they are following strangers.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In like manner, how many are praising within who will yet blaspheme; are chaste who will yet be fornicators; are sober who will wallow hereafter in drink; are standing who will by and by fall!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: These are not the sheep.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: (For we speak of those who were predestinated,—of those whom the Lord knoweth that they are His.)

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And yet these, so long as they keep right, listen to the voice of Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Yea, these hear, the others do not; and yet, according to predestination, these are not sheep, while the others are.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: There remains still the question, which I now think may meanwhile thus be solved.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: There is a voice of some kind,—there is, I say, a certain kind of voice of the Shepherd, in respect of which the sheep hear not strangers, and in respect of which those who are not sheep do not hear Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What a word is this!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “He that endureth to the end, the same shall be saved.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: No one of His own is indifferent to such a voice, a stranger does not hear it: for this reason also does He announce it to the former, that he may abide perseveringly with Himself to the end; but by one who is wanting in such persevering continuance with Him, such a word remains unheard.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: One has come to Christ, and has heard word after word of one kind and another, all of them true, all of them salutary; and among all the rest is also this utterance, “He that endureth to the end, the same shall be saved.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He who has heard this is one of the sheep.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But there was, perhaps, some one listening to it, who treated it with dislike, with coldness, and heard it as that of a stranger.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If he was predestinated, he strayed for the time, but he was not lost for ever: he returns to hear what he has neglected, to do what he has heard.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if he is one of those who are predestinated, then both his very wandering and his future conversion have been foreknown by God: if he has strayed away, he will return to hear that voice of the Shepherd, and to follow Him who saith, “He that endureth to the end, the same shall be saved.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: A good voice, brethren, it is; true and shepherd-like, the very voice of salvation in the tabernacles of the righteous.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For it is easy to hear Christ, easy to praise the gospel, easy to applaud the preacher: but to endure unto the end, is peculiar to the sheep who hear the Shepherd’s voice.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: A temptation befalls thee, endure thou to the end, for the temptation will not endure to the end.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And what is that end to which thou shalt endure?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Even till thou reachest the end of thy pathway.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For as long as thou hearest not Christ, He is thine adversary in the pathway, that is, in this mortal life.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And what doth He say?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Agree with thine adversary quickly, while thou art in the way with him.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou hast heard, hast believed, hast agreed.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If thou hast been at enmity, agree.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If thou hast got the opportunity of coming to an agreement, keep not up the quarrel longer.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For thou knowest not when thy way will be ended, and it is known to Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If thou art a sheep, and if thou endurest to the end, thou shalt be saved: and therefore it is that His own despise not that voice, and strangers hear it not.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: According to my ability, as He gave me the power, I have either explained to you or gone over with you a subject of great profundity.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If any have failed fully to understand, let him retain his piety, and the truth will be revealed: and let not those who have understood vaunt themselves as swifter at the expense of the slower, lest in their vaunting they turn out of the track, and the slower more easily attain the goal.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But let all of us be guided by Him to whom we say, “Lead me, O Lord, in Thy way, and I will walk in Thy truth.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: By this, then, which the Lord hath explained, that He Himself is the door, let us find entrance to what He has set forth, but not explained.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And indeed who it is that is the Shepherd, although He hath not told us in the lesson we have read to-day, yet in that which follows He very plainly tells us: “I am the good Shepherd.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And although He had not said so, whom else but Himself ought we to have understood in those words where He saith, “He that entereth in by the door is the Shepherd of the sheep.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To Him the porter openeth: and the sheep hear His voice: and He calleth His own sheep by name, and leadeth them out.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And when He putteth forth His own sheep, He goeth before them, and the sheep follow Him: for they know His voice”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For who else calleth His own sheep by name, and leadeth them hence unto eternal life, but He who knoweth the names of those that are fore-ordained?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hence He said to His disciples, “Rejoice that your names are written in heaven;” for from this it is that He calleth them by name.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And who else putteth them forth, save He who putteth away their sins, that, freed from their grievous fetters, they may be able to follow Him?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And who hath gone before them to the place whither they are to follow Him, but He who, rising from the dead, dieth no more; and death shall have no more dominion over Him; and who, when He was manifest here in the flesh, said, “Father, I will that they also whom Thou hast given me be with me where I am”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hence it is that He saith, “I am the door: by me if any man enter in, he shall be saved, and shall go in and out, and find pasture.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In this He clearly shows that not only the Shepherd, but the sheep also enter in by the door.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But what is this, “He shall go in and out, and find pasture”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To enter indeed into the Church by Christ the door, is eminently good; but to go out of the Church, as this same John the evangelist saith in his epistle, “They went out from us, but they were not of us,” is certainly otherwise than good.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Such a going out could not then be commended by the good Shepherd, when He said, “And he shall go in and out, and find pasture.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: There is therefore not only some sort of entrance, but some outgoing also that is good, by the good door, which is Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But what is that praiseworthy and blessed outgoing?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I might say, indeed, that we enter when we engage in some inward exercise of thought; and go out, when we take to some active work without: and since, as the apostle saith, Christ dwelleth in our hearts by faith, to enter by Christ is to give ourselves to thought in accordance with that faith; but to go out by Christ is, in accordance also with that same faith, to take to outside works, that is to say, in the presence of others.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hence, also, we read in a psalm, “Man goeth forth to his work;” and the Lord Himself saith, “Let your works shine before men.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But I am better pleased that the Truth Himself, like a good Shepherd, and therefore a good Teacher, hath in a certain measure reminded us how we ought to understand His words, “He shall go in and out, and find pasture,” when He added in the sequel, “The thief cometh not, but for to steal, and to kill, and to destroy: I am come that they might have life, and that they might have it more abundantly.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For He seems to me to have meant, That they may have life in coming in, and have it more abundantly at their departure.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For no one can pass out by the door—that is, by Christ—to that eternal life which shall be open to the sight, unless by the same door—that is, by the same Christ—he has entered His church, which is His fold, to the temporal life, which is lived in faith.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore, He saith, “I am come that they may have life,” that is, faith, which worketh by love; by which faith they enter the fold that they may live, for the just liveth by faith: “and that they may have it more abundantly,” who, enduring unto the end, pass out by this same door, that is, by the faith of Christ; for as true believers they die, and will have life more abundantly when they come whither the Shepherd hath preceded them, where they shall die no more.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Although, therefore, there is no want of pasture even here in the fold,—for we may understand the words “and shall find pasture” as referring to both, that is, both to their going in and their going out,—yet there only will they find the true pasture. where they shall be filled who hunger and thirst after righteousness,—such pasture as was found by him to whom it was said, “To-day shalt thou be with me in paradise.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But how He Himself is the door, and Himself the Shepherd, so that He also may in a certain respect be understood as going in and out by Himself, and who is the porter, it would be too long to inquire to-day, and, according to the grace given us by Himself, to unfold in the way of dissertation.
